title: Substitute ({key::s})
subtitle: The most powerful single command in Vim.
summary: :s/pattern/replacement/flags is the workhorse for text transformation. Combine with ranges (% for whole file) and flags (g for global, c for confirm, i for case-insensitive).

blocks.text:
{key::}{key:s} is Vim's find-and-replace. The full form is:

```
:[range]s/pattern/replacement/flags
```

Master it and you can rewrite a file in one keystroke.

rows: All matches in last visual selection
Replace **all** matches on each line (not just first)

blocks.title: What :s Actually Does
blocks.text:
`:s` runs the regex engine over each line in `[range]` exactly once (unless `g` flag), substituting matches in place. The buffer's lines are rewritten line-by-line; the cursor lands on the last line changed.

  • Without `[range]`, the range is the **current line only** (this trips up beginners — they expect file-wide and get one line).
  • The `%` range is shorthand for `1,$` (whole file).
  • The `c` flag enters an interactive prompt — `y` = yes, `n` = no, `a` = all (rest), `q` = quit, `l` = last (do this then quit).

**Re-running:** {key::}{key:&} repeats the last substitute on the current line. {key::}{key:%}{key:&} repeats on the whole file. {key:&} in *normal mode* = same as {key::}{key:&}.
{key::}{key:%}{key:s}{key://}{key:bar}{key:/}{key:g} reuses the last search pattern as the substitute pattern. Pair with {key:*} (search word under cursor) for a 5-keystroke replace-all.

blocks.title: Substitute is search-pattern + replacement-template + flags
blocks.text: {key::}{key:s} composes three subsystems: the regex engine (same one driving {key:/}); a small templating layer that expands `\0`, `\1`–`\9`, `&`, `~`, `\u`, `\U`, `\l`, `\L`, `\E`; and a flag-driven loop controller (`g`, `c`, `i`, `e`, `n`, `p`, `&`). Empty pattern reuses the last search — that's the global search-pattern slot again.
